title: Gauff Overcomes Illness to Win in Madrid
summary: Coco Gauff, the third seed from the United States, showed incredible determination as she fought back from a set and a break down to defeat Sorana Cirstea 4-6, 7-5, 6-1 in the third round of the Madrid Open. Despite battling a stomach virus that caused her to vomit into a bin during the second set, Gauff managed to rally and secure victory. She took a medical timeout and received medication that helped ease her symptoms. The win sets up a fourth-round match against Czech player Linda Noskova. Gauff is the defending champion at the French Open, which starts next month, and this tournament is part of her preparation on clay. Several other players have also been affected by illness in Madrid, including world number one Iga Swiatek, who retired from her match due to lack of energy. Meanwhile, British player Cameron Norrie defeated Thiago Agustin Tirante 7-5, 7-6(5) and will face world number one Jannik Sinner in the next round.

background_read: The Madrid Open is a professional tennis tournament played on clay courts. It is part of the ATP and WTA tours. Coco Gauff is a rising star who won the French Open in 2023. The French Open is one of four Grand Slam tournaments and is played on clay. Illness has affected several players this week, highlighting the physical demands of the tour.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Coco Gauff overcame a stomach virus to defeat Sorana Cirstea and advance to the fourth round of the Madrid Open. Purpose: To report on Gauff's victory and her preparation for the French Open. Evidence Evaluation: The article includes direct quotes from Gauff and details about the match, such as the score and her medical timeout. Author Credibility: BBC Sport is a reputable source for sports news. Methodology: The article is based on match observations and post-match interviews.
